description:
First wedge of the value-rep arc. lower_prim for BinOp::Add consults fn_types[var] for both operands. When both are statically `int`, emit a direct `iadd`; skip the tag-check fast/slow path entirely. Same for Sub/Mul/Div/Mod on int-int operands.

Frame layout stays tagged at this atom — slot 3 is still a tagged FzValue. Unbox / rebox happens only at op boundaries inside the fn body. Isolates the change to lower_prim; frame schemas, ABIs, GC tracer all untouched.

## Adds

  - lower_prim::lower_binop_int_int (or similar) emitting direct Cranelift arithmetic when both operand Descrs are Int.
  - VR.0 fixture asserting `fn add1: iadd` + `excludes icmp_imm`.

## Kills

  - The bxor_imm/icmp_imm/brif tag-check fast/slow path scaffolding emitted in lower_prim for Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod when both operands are typed int.
  - Reduced caller pressure on `fz_arith_*` FFI (still keep symbols alive — caller set narrows; full deletion is VR.2's job).

## Acceptance

  - `fz dump --emit clif fixtures/add1.fz` contains exactly one `iadd` with no surrounding tag check (VR.0 assertion).
  - Matrix stdout parity preserved across all paths.
  - At least one fixture using VR.0 keys.
title: VR.1: int+int unboxed arithmetic
